message:
fix: Correct genome track segment positioning in annotation overlays

When gene information is missing or the gene cannot be found in the phage
genome, genome track segments were defaulting to position 0 (start: 0, end: 0).
This caused annotations without valid gene associations to incorrectly stack
at the beginning of the genome track visualization, creating misleading displays.

Root cause: The useMemo hooks creating GenomeTrackSegment arrays used nullish
coalescing (gene?.startPos ?? 0) which defaulted missing positions to zero
rather than filtering out invalid entries.

Fix: Changed the segment generation logic to:
1. Check if geneId exists before attempting gene lookup
2. Skip segments where the gene cannot be found
3. Use filter() to remove null entries from the final array
4. Use TypeScript type predicate to maintain type safety

This ensures only annotations with valid, mappable gene positions appear on
the genome track, providing accurate spatial visualization.

Files changed:
- AMGPathwayOverlay.tsx: Filter AMG segments without valid gene positions
- ProteinDomainOverlay.tsx: Filter domain segments without valid gene positions
- DefenseArmsRaceOverlay.tsx: Filter defense system segments without valid
  gene positions (also includes hotkey change Alt+R → Alt+E to resolve
  conflict with StructureConstraintOverlay)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
